brand.name: Pinnacle Investments
brand.tagline: We Buy Mortgage Notes, Trust Deeds, and Contracts Nationwide!

offer.what: Purchases seller-financed mortgage notes, trust deeds, land contracts, and promissory notes from private note holders — both performing and non-performing — in exchange for a lump-sum cash payment. Partial note purchases also available.
offer.coreOutcome: Immediate liquidity and freedom from the administrative burden of collecting monthly payments, chasing late payers, and managing note paperwork.
offer.pricePoint: Custom per-note offer (no published pricing); note holders receive a discounted lump sum based on down payment, interest rate, term, payer credit, and property condition. Ticket sizes vary widely based on note balance — typically tens to hundreds of thousands of dollars.
offer.commitment: One-time transaction; no ongoing relationship required. Partial-note options allow staged liquidity.

audience.icp: Private individuals (often homeowners or real estate investors) who owner-financed a property sale and are now holding the resulting mortgage note — receiving monthly payments but wanting or needing immediate cash. Nationwide, skews toward retirees and older property owners.
painPoints: Capital is locked in a note while life needs cash now (retirement, medical bills, college tuition, taxes)
Tired of chasing monthly payments and managing paperwork
Fear of payer default leaving them exposed
Unsure how much their note is actually worth
Uncertainty about the process of selling a note and whether it is safe or legitimate
segments: Retirees seeking lump-sum income from a held note
Note holders with urgent financial events (medical, education, inheritance planning)
Experienced real estate investors holding multiple notes wanting to redeploy capital
Accidental note holders (inherited or received in a settlement) who want to simplify finances

salesCycle.length: Days to weeks — closing typically 10–15 working days once offer is accepted
salesCycle.trial: Yes — free, no-obligation note analysis / written quote as the entry point
salesCycle.closeMechanism: Phone/email consultation with an advisor leads to a customized written offer; deal closes through a title company with funds via cashier's check or wire transfer
salesCycle.topObjection: Will I get fair market value for my note, or is the discount too steep? / Is this company trustworthy?

ahaMoment: Receiving a concrete written offer with a real dollar amount — the moment the note holder sees liquidity is real and attainable within two weeks.

retention.recurring: Primarily one-time transactions; low inherent repeat rate per client
retention.expansion: Partial note sales (sell portions of the note stream over time); clients with multiple notes; referrals to other note holders in their network
retention.churnRisk: Prospect shops the quote against competitors and takes a higher bid elsewhere; quote requesters who go cold after initial inquiry

lifecycleGaps: Welcome & Onboarding — likely no formal sequence after quote request; strong gap
Lead-Magnet Nurture — no evident follow-up drip after Note Holder's Handbook download or free analysis request
Trial/Consult Conversion — converting free-quote requesters who haven't accepted an offer yet
Value Newsletter — no evidence of educational content email program
Win-Back / Re-Engagement — no evidence of re-engagement for cold quote prospects
Referral / Advocacy — 22,000+ past transactions with no visible referral program
toneVoice: Professional, authoritative, and reassuring. Credibility-forward (26 years, 22,000+ transactions, 8 certified planners). Plain-spoken and educational rather than salesy — the audience is financially unsophisticated about note markets and needs trust built before acting. Warm but not casual.
compliance: Financial services — subject to CAN-SPAM/CASL for email. Note purchasing is not a securities activity but may carry state-specific disclosure requirements for financial transactions. No HIPAA. Should avoid language that constitutes investment advice. Disclosures on email footers re: not being a licensed broker-dealer are prudent.
